name: Rajas De Chile Poblano (Chile Poblano Strips)
recipeInstructions:
In the central and northern part of Mexico, where the chile poblano is used a great deal, to ask for rajas most commonly means strips of chile poblano cooked in this way. It makes an excellent accompaniment for broiled meats.
Remove the seeds and veins from the chiles and cut them into strips about 1 1/2 inches long and 1/2 inch wide. If the chiles seem too spicy, soak them in salted water for about 30 minutes.
Heat the oil over medium heat and fry the onions gently, without browning them, until they are soft.
Add the chile strips and salt to the onions in the pan. Stir occasionally and cook gently for about 8 minutes.
You can prepare these ahead of time and store them in the refrigerator for several days. They tend to get a bit soggy if frozen.
